title: carrissoa angolensis
intro: Carrissoa angolensis, also known as the Angola tree, is a medicinal plant species found in the tropical regions of Central and West Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of Carrissoa angolensis include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as the prevention of malaria and other diseases. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine by local communities in its native regions.
